title: Iran free-market USD/IRR rate, 1999-2002, secondary-compiled cross-check
source_org: Wikipedia (Iranian rial article), cross-referenced against Iran Primer/USIP framing of the same multi-tier era
failures: Iran Primer USIP page (iranprimer.usip.org) returned a certificate-expired error, could not cross-verify directly -- flagged, not silently dropped
license_terms: Wikipedia CC BY-SA -- treated per this project's established convention of using Wikipedia only as a cross-check pending primary-source verification, not as a standalone primary citation. A follow-up agent is tasked with tracing these figures to their underlying primary source (likely IMF Article IV or a Bank Melli/CBI bulletin).
notes: These are genuinely different numbers from the same years' WDI PA.NUS.FCRF 'preferred/official' rate (1999: ~1750 official vs 9430 free-market -- more than 5x divergence), confirming the multi-tier system's real-world effect. Cross-validated against this project's own 2011 TGJU data (13568-13700), which sits on a plausible continuation of this trend.
